assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan's 'Existing Code Inspection' section explicitly lists all 4 files as inspected: (1) advisory/endpoints/get.rs for endpoint pattern, (2) advisory/service/advisory.rs for service method structure, (3) advisory/model/summary.rs for model pattern, and (4) common/src/error.rs for AppError and .context() usage.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan lists exactly 3 files to create (severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint, advisory_summary.rs test) and 3 files to modify (advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs). assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md commit message: 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation service and endpoint' (Conventional Commits format). Footer includes 'Refs: TC-9201'.

assertion_results.evidence: plan.md commit strategy section includes '--trailer="Assisted-by: Claude Code"' in the commit command.

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md identifies 9 conventions including: (1) 'Error Handling: Result<T, AppError> with .context() Wrapping' and (2) 'Module Structure: model/ + service/ + endpoints/ Pattern'. Also identifies naming conventions, endpoint handler patterns, route registration, service method patterns, model struct patterns, test patterns, and module registration.
